g.data-narration: Welcome to Java Comments! Learn about documentation that makes code maintainable and collaborative.
Comments are non-executable statements ignored by the compiler. They provide internal documentation within source code for developers.
g.text: Non-executable statements in Java that provide documentation within source code.
Ignored completely by the Java compiler during compilation.
Help developers understand, maintain, and debug programs effectively.

g.data-narration: Comments improve code readability, maintainability, and enable team collaboration. They explain the 'why' behind code logic.
Single-line comments start with // and extend to the end of the line. Use them for brief explanations about specific code lines.
g.text: Begin with // and continue to end of the line
int age = 25; // This describes the variable

g.data-narration: Common uses include explaining variables, debugging by disabling code, and adding quick notes about specific operations.
Multi-line comments start with /* and end with */. Everything between is ignored by the compiler - perfect for longer explanations.
g.text: Start with /* and end with */ - can span multiple lines

g.data-narration: Use multi-line comments for function descriptions, algorithm explanations, and commenting out code blocks during debugging.
g.text: Takes length and width as parameters */

g.data-narration: Documentation comments begin with /** and end with */. The Javadoc tool generates HTML documentation from these special comments.
g.text: Special multi-line comments for generating external API documentation

g.data-narration: Javadoc uses @author for the developer's name and @version for program version. These tags appear at class-level documentation.
Use @param to describe method parameters and @return to explain the return value. Essential for method documentation.
Other useful Javadoc tags include @since for version introduced, @throws for exceptions, and @see for cross-references.
Important: Java does NOT support nested multi-line comments! Attempting to nest /* */ comments will cause a compilation error.
g.text: Java does NOT support nested multi-line comments - will cause compilation error

g.data-narration: Follow best practices: explain WHY not WHAT, keep comments updated, avoid obvious statements, and use proper grammar.
Here's a complete example demonstrating all three comment types in a well-documented Java class.
Quick reference: single-line uses //, multi-line uses /* */, documentation uses /** */ with @ tags. All are ignored by compiler.
For exams: remember syntax differences, Javadoc generates HTML, no nested comments, and demonstrate good commenting practices for bonus marks!
g.text: • Remember all three comment syntaxes • Javadoc tags: @author, @version, @param, @return
• Comments have ZERO effect on execution • No nested comments in Java • Proper comments earn bonus marks!
